Title: Big Caring unveils automated Klang distribution centre and HQ
Article:
KLANG: Big Caring Group has launched a new distribution centre in Klang, alongside a centralised corporate headquarters, to strengthen its supply chain through technology innovation.
As one of Malaysia's largest retail pharmacy and healthcare networks, it operates more than 600 stores under BIG, CARiNG, Georgetown, Ting and Wellings brands.
Its network includes 5,000 healthcare partner touchpoints across hospitals, clinics and physiotherapy centres.
The facility handles over 90,000 order lines daily, reflecting growing scale across its operations.
Big Caring executive director Lim Sin Yin said the automated distribution centre was built from Covid-19 lessons, where demand volatility and supply chain strain exposed the limits of traditional models and efficiency alone was insufficient.
"We believe that strengthening our operations and supply chain is just as important as delivering care itself. It begins with how we plan demand, anticipate needs, and position supply.
"This distribution centre is a high-performance engine that connects planning to execution, allowing us to manage complexity with greater control and structure.
"At this scale, efficiency is not optional, but a capability and responsibility that we must deliver on every day," he said during a media visit to Big Caring's new corporate headquarters and distribution centre here yesterday.
The facility integrates robotics, warehouse systems and inventory platforms into an automation ecosystem.
Its proximity to Port Klang improves inbound efficiency and nationwide distribution reliability.
Upstream, artificial intelligence-driven smart replenishment strengthens demand planning.
Systems analyse real-time sales, anticipate demand shifts and optimise inventory across the network.
Downstream solutions include tote-to-person and shelf-to-person systems for different speeds.
More than 160 robots and 700 racks support storage density and faster fulfilment.
Picking efficiency improved 76.5 per cent, with 99.8 per cent accuracy.
Warehouse transformation is also seen in workforce operations.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: China hopes 'relevant parties' can grasp chance at peace in Iran war
Article:
BEIJING: China's foreign ministry said it hoped the "relevant parties can grasp this chance at peace and bring the region back to stability as soon as possible" after the US and Iran agreed to a ceasefire in the weeks-long Middle East war.
China said it has maintained communication with all parties and "actively sought to promote reconciliation and prevent further fighting," foreign ministry spokesperson Mao Ning said in a daily press briefing.
Late on Tuesday, both the US and Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire to the conflict that has rattled global markets and sparked geopolitical upheaval.
Beijing hoped that relevant parties would seize the opportunity for peace, "resolve differences through dialogue and consultation and work towards to early restoration of peace and stability in the Gulf and the Middle East region," Mao said.
US President Donald Trump vowed to retain military assets in the Middle East until a peace deal with Iran is reached ​and warned of a major escalation in fighting if it failed to comply with the terms of the ceasefire. Oil prices rose again on concerns over supply and fears restrictions in the Strait of Hormuz will remain for now. - Reuters
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Data centre boom may strain renewable financing, says CIMB Islamic
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The rapid expansion of data centres could constrain financing for renewable energy projects unless Malaysia updates its long-term energy transition assumptions, CIMB Islamic Bank Bhd sustainable finance head Arshad Nuval Othman said.
He said the National Energy Transition Roadmap (NETR) financing projections were formulated before the recent surge in data centre investments, which has significantly increased electricity demand and funding requirements for renewable power.
"The financing requirements we previously assessed were based on the NETR up to 2050, but that was before data centres came into the picture.
"Data centres have changed the energy demand profile. If we don't adjust it, we risk running out of headroom to finance the energy transition," he said.
Speaking at the Malaysia Energy, Water and Climate Change Summit 2026, Arshad said banks are also constrained by single counterparty exposure limits, which cap lending at 25 per cent of a bank's equity.
"This could restrict financing for large-scale energy projects. Two years ago, Bank Negara Malaysia accepted my recommendation to increase limits for the banking system.
"When the NETR was introduced, the data centre boom was not yet apparent. We need to adjust it. The financial system will recalibrate, as it has done before," he added.
He said Bank Negara remains receptive to policy changes, allowing banks to recalibrate lending once updated energy demand projections are available.
While financing capacity may need to be revised, Arshad said funding availability itself is not a concern, given Malaysia's well-developed banking and capital markets.
However, he cautioned that perceptions around coal prices and existing infrastructure could delay renewable investment, even though renewables are cheaper and faster to deploy.
"You might think that with rising oil and gas prices, it is convenient to fall back on coal, both in terms of cost and existing infrastructure.
"But this is a perception. Renewable energy infrastructure is cheaper and can be deployed within six months," he said.
Looking ahead, Arshad said renewable energy targets under the NETR should remain unchanged, even as capacity assumptions are updated to reflect rising demand from data centres.
"What must not change is our renewable energy mix. By 2030, the target is 30 per cent renewable energy, and by 2050, 70 per cent.
"It is a matter of updating capacity requirements to account for data centre demand," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MTT Shipping retail IPO oversubscribed 2.7 times ahead of listing
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: MTT Shipping and Logistics Bhd's retail initial public offering (IPO) tranche was oversubscribed by 2.70 times ahead of its Main Market listing on April 21.
The offering drew 10,863 applications seeking 184.79 million shares worth RM190.33 million, against 50 million shares allocated to the Malaysian public.
The Bumiputera public portion received 3,893 applications for 54.14 million shares, or 1.17 times oversubscribed, while the other public portion was oversubscribed by 4.23 times after receiving 6,970 applications for 130.65 million shares.
Shares reserved for eligible persons were fully subscribed, while those allocated via private placement to selected investors were fully placed out, the company said.
In a statement, MTT Shipping said shares reserved for Bumiputera investors approved by the Investment, Trade and Industry Ministry (Miti) were also fully taken up.
Notices of allotment will be issued to successful applicants by April 17.
The IPO comprises 633.5 million shares priced at RM1.03 each, including an institutional offering of 571 million shares to Malaysian and foreign investors, as well as Bumiputera investors approved by Miti, and a retail offering of 62.5 million shares to directors, employees, contributors and the Malaysian public.
CIMB Investment Bank is the principal adviser, joint global coordinator, joint bookrunner, managing underwriter and joint underwriter for the IPO.
CLSA Ltd and CLSA Securities Malaysia Sdn Bhd are the joint global coordinators and joint bookrunners, while Affin Hwang Investment Bank Bhd is the joint bookrunner and joint underwriter.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Zahid: Cloud-seeding operation in Perak a success
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The cloud-seeding operations (OPAs) implemented in Perak yesterday have shown positive development, with rainfall recorded in several key water catchment areas in the state, said Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi.
He said that a significant amount of rainfall - between 29.3 millimetres (mm) and 84.8mm - was recorded in the Bukit Merah Dam and Temenggor Dam areas.
"This provides an early boost to the dams' water levels, which were previously affected by dry weather," he posted on his Facebook page today.
He said the success proved that the science-based approach and close monitoring by the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) and the Royal Malaysian Air Force (RMAF) have had a clear impact within a short period.
Zahid, who is also the National Disaster Management Committee chairman, said that operations would continue today in the southern parts of the peninsula, involving several main dams in Johor and Melaka.
He said the operations in the south would focus on the Machap Dam, Upper Layang Dam, Lower Layang Dam, Lebam Dam and Pulai Dam in Johor and the Durian Tunggal Dam and Jus Dam in Melaka.
"We will continue to monitor requirements from time to time and ensure every move taken has the best effect on water supply, the agriculture sector and the people's well-being.
"Let us all pray that these efforts will be facilitated by Allah , and blessed with good results for all," he added.
Earlier, MetMalaysia said the operations' effectiveness was supported by high atmospheric moisture content and unstable atmospheric conditions, thereby boosting the development of rain clouds following the seeding process
The cloud seeding process at the Bukit Merah Dam and Temenggor Dam yesterday was conducted using RMAF C-130 Hercules aircraft, and the technique involved dispersing salt solutions into towering cumulus cloud formations at altitudes between 5,200 feet and 7,000 feet.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Mercedes reports drop in Q1 sales during 'transition year' for China market
Article:
BERLIN: Mercedes-Benz reported another drop in sales in its core car business in the first quarter of 2026 as the German premium carmaker lost further ground in the tough Chinese market amid an overhaul of its lineup there.
Quarterly sales fell six per cent to 419,400 vehicles from the same quarter a year ago, it said in a statement on Thursday.
While sales volumes grew by seven per cent in Europe and 20 per cent in the United States, this was not enough to offset a 27 per cent plunge in China.
Mercedes and its rival BMW face a cut-throat price war with local brands in China, the world's biggest auto market.
Mercedes said 2026 would be a "transition year" for the brand in China, with the decline partly driven by the phase-out of models in its entry segment ahead of the launch of new models.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Asian equities slip on doubts over durability of Mideast ceasefire
Article:
HONG KONG: Emerging Asian shares retreated on Thursday as new fault lines between the US and Iran raised concerns over the durability of a fragile Middle East ceasefire, exacerbating fears about the potential impact on economies exposed to oil shocks.
The MSCI gauge of EM Asia equities slipped about one per cent from a one-month high scaled the prior day, dragged by a 1.6 per cent drop in South Korea's KOSPI.
An index tracking global EM stocks eased 0.9 per cent from a five-week high as oil futures rose with energy flows through the Strait of Hormuz staying restricted as Iran flexed control over the vital oil artery.
Israel pounded Lebanon with its heaviest strikes yet on Wednesday, killing hundreds of people and drawing a threat of retaliation from Iran, which suggested it would be "unreasonable" to proceed with talks to forge a permanent peace deal with the US
"The cracks that are appearing in this latest ceasefire agreement are mostly due to the mixed messaging from the parties involved," said Isaac Lim, chief markets strategist at online trading platform Moomoo.
"In the event of a breakdown before the two weeks are up, oil export dependent countries like South Korea, Thailand, Malaysia and Philippines will be required to dig deeper into their reserves and, in worse case scenario, ask for external help from both a physical reserve and funding perspective."
Energy-dependent Asian economies are exposed to oil price volatility, which threatens to stoke inflation and strain economic growth. Shares in Kuala Lumpur fell 0.8 per cent, while stocks in Singapore slid about 0.4 per cent and those in Jakarta were flat.
Currencies in the region were also on the back foot. The Indonesian rupiah slipped to 17,090 a dollar, retesting the 17,100 mark it touched earlier in the week, while the Philippine peso weakened 0.5 per cent to 59.729 per dollar.
In Thailand, stocks extended their winning streak to a third day, edging higher to hover near a five-week high. The baht remained anchored around a one-month high of 32.06 a dollar it touched the previous day.
Recently re-elected Thai Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul outlined his government's policy agenda to parliament with a focus on growth, debt relief and measures to reduce business costs.
The Bank of Thailand said it will keep policy rates on hold, while it monitors inflation risks. Earlier this week, data showed that Thailand's inflation unexpectedly dipped in March. The Philippine stock market was closed for a holiday.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: ACE Market hopeful Inspace Creation in underwriting deal with TA Securities
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Inspace Creation Bhd has signed an underwriting agreement with TA Securities Holdings Bhd for its initial public offering (IPO) on Bursa Malaysia's ACE Market.
In a statement today, the interior fitting-out services provider said TA Securities will underwrite 26.97 million new shares under the exercise.
The IPO involves the issuance of 68.50 million new ordinary shares, or 18.55 per cent of the enlarged issued share capital of 369.30 million ordinary shares, and an offer for sale of 29.30 million existing shares, or 7.93 per cent.
Of the total new shares, 18.47 million will be made available to the Malaysian public, while 8.50 million will be allocated to eligible directors, employees and other contributors. Another 41.53 million will be placed out to selected investors.
The offer for sale of 29.30 million existing shares will also be undertaken via private placement to selected investors.
Executive director Wong Chong Siong said the underwriting agreement marks a key step in the group's listing journey.
"Our IPO will provide the necessary financial resources to strengthen our position and enable us to undertake more projects or projects of higher value.
"This strategic move supports our expansion into new commercial segments and regional markets beyond the Klang Valley," he said.
Inspace Creation also said that as at July 28, 2025, it maintains an unbilled order book of RM21.22 million from its interior fitting-out services.
The company has completed more than 100 projects to date, with a total contract value of about RM150 million.
TA Securities will act as the principal adviser, sponsor, sole placement agent and sole underwriter for the IPO.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Penang scores above 89 per cent in Urban Sustainability Index
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: Penang has recorded an overall score of between 89.13 and 90.32 per cent in the 2025 Urban Sustainability Index (USI) released by the Housing and Local Government Ministry.
The achievement was announced at the 50th National Physical Planning Council (MPFN) meeting chaired by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim at Perdana Putra here today.
USI is a framework used to evaluate how cities perform across environmental, social and economic dimensions, especially in addressing rapid urban growth and the challenges posed by climate change
Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said both local authorities — Penang Island City Council (MBPP) and Seberang Prai City Council (MBSP) — attained sustainable status based on assessments across key dimensions.
He said these include competitive economic performance, environmental sustainability, optimal land use and natural resources, efficient infrastructure and transport as well as effective governance, all of which achieved full sustainable ratings.
He, however, noted that the Prosperous Community dimension registered only a moderately sustainable score, ranging between 0.50 and 0.79.
"In terms of overall performance, 42 indicators recorded sustainable scores above 0.80, six were moderately sustainable (0.50 to 0.79), while three fell below 0.49, placing them in the less sustainable category," he said after the meeting.
Also present were Deputy Prime Ministers Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi and Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof, Housing and Local Government Minister Nga Kor Ming along with other state leaders.
Chow said the state would prioritise improvements in nine indicators that have yet to meet sustainable benchmarks.
"These include the poverty rate in Seberang Prai, the provision of affordable housing, the ratio of secondary schools to population and the availability of government and private preschools.
"Other areas identified for improvement are public nuisance complaints, the percentage of food premises with Grade A ratings, the number of five-star public toilets and per capita daily domestic water consumption.
"The state government and relevant technical agencies must take targetted action to address these gaps," he added.
Chow said focus and timely measures are needed to ensure these indicators improve and Penang's overall sustainability performance continues to strengthen.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: #NSTviral: Factory bus rams vehicles on 2nd Penang Bridge
Article:
BATU KAWAN: A video has gone viral
showing a factory bus ramming into several vehicles
on the Sultan Abdul Halim Mu'adzam Shah Bridge.
The incident is believed to have occurred at 2.20pm on March 23.
The one-minute footage shows the factory bus first colliding with a red car and then a lorry, before all three vehicles came to a stop by the roadside.
It is understood that the video was recorded by a car following the factory bus.
Preliminary reports indicate that no one was injured in the incident.
The New Straits Times has contacted Penang police for comment and is awaiting their response.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Blogger Papagomo faces legal action over false claims against MCMC
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) will take legal action against Wan Muhammad Azri Wan Deris, known as "Papagomo", over alleged false claims involving the commission.
In a statement today, MCMC denied that it had blocked a Malaysia Gazette report on his court case involving Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil.
"MCMC emphasises that the allegation is untrue. The commission was not involved and did not request any media or platform to block the content," it said.
The commission said it takes the matter seriously and will pursue legal action against Wan Azri over the claims.
MCMC also said it takes a firm stance against the misuse of social media and digital platforms to spread false information.
"Strict action will be taken against anyone found to have violated existing laws," it added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Israel military claims it has killed nephew of Hizbollah chief Naim Qassem
Article:
JERUSALEM: Israel's military said Thursday it had struck and killed a close adviser to Hizbollah chief Naim Qassem in Beirut a day earlier, when Israeli strikes pummelled Lebanon.
"Yesterday, the IDF struck in the Beirut area and eliminated Ali Yusuf Harshi, the personal secretary and nephew of Hizbollah Secretary-General Naim Qassem," a military statement said.
Harshi was "a close associate and personal adviser to... Qassem and played a central role in managing and securing his office", it added. - AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Spain condemns Israeli attacks on Lebanon, reopens Teheran embassy
Article:
MADRID: Spain's Foreign Minister Jose Manuel Albares on Thursday accused Israel of violating international law and a newly brokered two-week ceasefire in the Middle East by carrying out airstrikes on Lebanon on Wednesday.
Spain has emerged as one of the most vocal critics among Western nations of US and Israeli actions in Iran and Lebanon, closing its airspace to any aircraft involved in a conflict Madrid has called reckless and illegal.
"Yesterday we saw how Israel, flouting the ceasefire and in violation of international law, dropped hundreds of bombs on Lebanon," Albares told lawmakers in the lower house.
Earlier on Thursday, Albares announced that Spain would reopen its embassy in Teheran in hopes of achieving peace in the region. "I've instructed our ambassador in Teheran to return, to take up his post again and reopen our embassy, and for us to join in this effort for peace from every possible quarter, including from the Iranian capital itself," Albares told reporters. — REUTERS
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 4 suspects surrender in vicious Triang attack, 2 still at large
Article:
BERA: Four men wanted over a brutal slashing in Triang last month have surrendered to police.
The suspects, aged 27 to 37, including three whose images were previously released, turned themselves in at Bera district police headquarters between April 6 and yesterday.
Bera police chief Superintendent Zulkiflee Nazir said that, to date, five suspects have been arrested, while police are tracking two others.
"Four suspects have been remanded, including one arrested at Kuala Lumpur Hospital on March 4. Three are remanded until April 13, the fourth until April 11," he added.
(https://www.nst.com.my/news/regional/2026/04/1410844/cops-nab-bera-slashing-suspect-hkl-ward)
"The fifth suspect, who surrendered yesterday, has been released on police bail due to health issues," he said.
Police are tracking two men, identified as Maran and Logathevan, to assist in the investigation into the attack, which left a 35-year-old seriously injured.
On April 5, Zulkiflee said police had identified four main suspects and were seeking three others who could help with inquiries.
The incident, which went viral, saw the victim
slashed with a parang
inside a restaurant at 9.15pm on March 29.
The victim has regained consciousness at Sultan Haji Ahmad Shah Hospital in Temerloh and was moved to a general ward on April 2.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Woman charged again with 44 counts of CBT involving more than RM1.3mil
Article:
MUAR: A former accounts executive of a wet tissue supply company was charged again at the Sessions Court here today, this time with 44 counts of criminal breach of trust (CBT) involving more than RM1.3 million in company funds.
The accused, Goh Hui Lan, 48, pleaded not guilty to all charges before Sessions Court judge Mohd Khairi Haron.
It is understood that today marked the final day of proceedings, bringing the total number of CBT charges against her to 131, involving losses amounting to RM3.6 million.
According to the charges, the accused allegedly deceived a 34-year-old female company manager into approving payments totalling RM1,352,954.28, which were purportedly meant for suppliers.
The offences were allegedly committed between Feb 25, 2020 and Nov 18, 2021 at her workplace in Parit Hulu, Bukit Bakri here, and were framed under Section 420 of the Penal Code.
The prosecution was led by deputy public prosecutor Diyana Najihah Muhamed Fauzi, while the accused was unrepresented. Lawyer Lim Shin Yi held a watching brief for the company.
The court allowed bail at RM160,000 for all charges and set May 21 for mention for document submission and appointment of counsel.
This brings the total bail imposed on the accused to RM460,000.
However, she was unable to post bail and was remanded at Simpang Renggam Prison pending further proceedings.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Suspect in Machang police attack remanded for seven days
Article:
KOTA BARU: A suspect arrested for allegedly attempting to kill a policeman in Machang last night has been remanded for seven days starting today.
Kelantan police chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said police obtained the remand order when the suspect was produced at the magistrate's court here today.
He said the suspect, who has nine prior criminal records, is being investigated under Section 307 of the Penal Code for attempted murder.
Yusoff confirmed that
the incident
occurred in front of Machang campus Universiti Teknologi MARA (UiTM) at 11.17pm last night.
"Police received information regarding a shooting at the location, prompting a team from the Machang police station who were on crime prevention patrol to be dispatched to the scene.
"During checks on two men found in suspicious circumstances, one of them turned aggressive and attacked police personnel with a parang.
"In self-defence and to avoid serious injury, the officer discharged several shots towards the suspect," he said.
Yusoff said the suspect fled the scene but was later apprehended in an immediate operation conducted in the vicinity.
"His accomplice managed to escape and efforts to track him down are ongoing," he said.
Yusoff said the suspect sustained injuries to his right thigh and is currently receiving treatment at Machang Hospital.
"His condition is reported to be stable," he said.
Police also seized a parang believed to have been used in the incident.
"Initial investigations revealed that the suspect, in his 30s, has at least nine crime records including those related to drugs, criminal intimidation, possession of dangerous weapons, and obstructing a public servant," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Temporary road closure on Jalan Yew for bridge reconstruction works
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The route along the Jalan Yew bridge will be temporarily closed to all vehicles from 10pm to 5am from April 10 to 21.
Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) said in a statement today that the closure is to facilitate girder launching works and the reconstruction of the bridge.
The closure involves the stretch between TRX and Bulatan Pudu, as well as parts of Jalan Cochrane and Jalan Pasar (from the Masjid Alam Shah junction to the Petron station on Jalan Pasar).
"During this period, road users are advised to use alternative routes via Jalan Pudu, Jalan Chan Sow Lin and Bulatan Kampung Pandan to reduce congestion and ensure smoother traffic flow.
"All road users are also urged to comply with traffic signs and the instructions of traffic personnel at the location to ensure safety and smooth travel," the statement said.
Members of the public with enquiries may contact 014-8042486 for further information. – BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Energy, water tariff reforms needed as subsidy burden rises, says Fadillah
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Subsidies for energy and water are increasingly straining long-term fiscal sustainability and system viability, prompting greater urgency to reform tariff structures, said Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof.
Fadillah, who is also the Energy Transition and Water Transformation Minister, said subsidies may provide short-term relief but risk creating structural burdens that could hinder investment.
"Subsidies may soften the present, but burden the future," he said at the Malaysia Energy, Water and Climate Change Summit 2026 today, adding that a sustainable system must reflect real costs.
Fadillah said Malaysia is seeing rising electricity demand driven by digitalisation, data centres and industrial growth, but the national grid was not designed to accommodate such rapid expansion.
"We face a delicate balance in transitioning away from fossil fuels while safeguarding energy security," he said, cautioning that the Middle East crisis and volatile oil prices should not distract from long-term vulnerabilities.
Fadillah said Tenaga Nasional Bhd will invest about RM42 billion starting this year to upgrade the grid and support renewable energy growth.
Malaysia has set a clear path towards net-zero emissions by 2050 and a 70 per cent renewable energy share in the power mix.
While energy powers the economy, water sustainability remains one of the country's most under-addressed challenges.
"Malaysia loses over 30 per cent of treated water. In some states, more than 40 per cent. This is not inefficiency, it is loss at scale," the minister said, adding that the establishment of his ministry reflects the urgency of reform.
"No other country has a specific ministry focused on energy transition and water transformation."
Among the key challenges, Fadillah said water tariffs remain disconnected from operational costs, limiting utilities' ability to maintain infrastructure and invest in upgrades.
This is despite available technologies such as smart metering and artificial intelligence-based leak detection, which often require significant capital investment.
"Malaysia cannot finance this transition alone. Public funds must catalyse, not carry the transition. When systems are clear, trust follows. When trust follows, investment flows," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Govt to test incentive-driven scheme to ease KL morning congestion
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The government is launching a behavioural-based initiative to tackle Kuala Lumpur's persistent morning congestion, encouraging commuters to enter the city earlier through targeted incentives.
Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh said that the programme, named Bangun Kuala Lumpur! (Bangun KL!), aims to redistribute peak-hour traffic by shifting commuter habits.
"A good city is not just one that looks attractive, but one that functions well for daily life. This is not about slogans, but about changing how the city moves every morning," she said at the launch.
Under the initiative, the government has partnered with Zus Coffee to roll out the "Masuk Kuala Lumpur Awal" campaign, offering discounted beverages to those who arrive in the city earlier.
A total of 250 outlets across Kuala Lumpur and Putrajaya are participating, with selected drinks, including Americano and latte, priced from as low as RM5 — representing discounts of nearly 30 per cent for purchases made between 7am and 8am via the Zus mobile application.
Yeoh said the initiative reflected a broader effort to improve urban liveability by helping Malaysians reclaim time otherwise lost in traffic.
"The role of government is not only to build infrastructure, but to enable people to regain their time — time for family, for health, and for a better quality of life," she said.
She added that about 1.2 million vehicles enter Kuala Lumpur each morning, with peak congestion occurring between 8am and 9am.
"If all vehicles enter within that one-hour window, congestion is inevitable. However, if even 10 per cent of commuters shift to arrive earlier, traffic flow will improve significantly," she said at the launch held at the Zus Coffee outlet on Jalan Hang Kasturi.
Also present were Federal Territory Sports Council chairman Datuk Stuart Michael Ramalingam and Zus Coffee group chief operating officer Venon Tian.
Yeoh added that even a modest reduction involving several thousand vehicles would positively impact the overall traffic system.
The programme, which will run throughout the year, will also allow the government to collect data on user behaviour, adoption rates, and the effectiveness of such incentives in reducing traffic jams.
She said similar collaborations could be extended to other sectors, including fitness operators, as part of a broader push to encourage off-peak activities and more flexible routines.
"Big changes do not always begin with big projects. They often start with small decisions made consistently by many people," she added.
Yeoh said that the initiative was voluntary and not intended to compel behavioural change, but rather to provide practical incentives for those willing to adjust their routines.
"The coffee promotion is just one example of how incentives could encourage people to start their day earlier. This is not about asking everyone to drink coffee, but about offering added value to those who already do," she said.
She also said the government planned to introduce a special programme for senior citizens, ensuring they feel valued in the capital.
"At this stage, we want city residents to realise that there are many privileges in Kuala Lumpur for those who start their day early," she said.
The Bangun KL! initiative marks the second phase of the administration's efforts in Kuala Lumpur and Putrajaya, shifting focus from improving access to public services and digital platforms in its first 100 days to enhancing lifestyle and overall quality of life in the capital.
The programme will run from April 9 until year-end across 250 participating outlets.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Man found dead in Mercedes died of heart attack
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A 66-year-old man was found dead in his car in front of a restaurant in Batu Caves on Tuesday.
Gombak police chief Assistant Commissioner Noor Ariffin Mohamad Nasir said the man died of a heart attack.
Earlier, photos and video clips went viral on social media showing the man unconscious in the driver's seat of a Mercedes-Benz.
It is understood that the deceased was a former editor of a local English news portal.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Fifth suspect held in RM12,000 Kelantan police bribery probe
Article:
KOTA BARU: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has arrested a man and obtained a five-day remand over a RM12,000 bribery case involving four policemen in Kelantan.
According to a source, the suspect, a man in his 30s, was detained at his home in the district at about 5pm yesterday.
"The latest arrest raises the total number of people held in connection with the case to five," the source said.
MACC today obtained a five-day remand order against the suspect at the Magistrate's Court.
The remand, which ends on April 13, was issued by Magistrate Rais Imran Hamid.
Kelantan MACC director Azmin Yusoff confirmed the arrest and said the case is being investigated under Section 16(a)(B) of the MACC Act 2009.
The suspect is believed to have conspired with four Tanah Merah district police officers who were remanded for five days on Tuesday.
"He is also believed to have received the bribe through transfers into his personal bank account," the source said.
The bribery case took place last year.
All four are believed to have
received the money in exchange
for not acting against a man found with prohibited items in his vehicle.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Israeli attacks on Lebanon violate US-Iran ceasefire, says Iranian minister
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Israeli attacks on Lebanon yesterday constitute a "grave violation" of the United States-Iran ceasefire, Iran's Deputy Foreign Minister Saeed Khatibzadeh told the BBC.
In an interview on the BBC's Today programme, Khatibzadeh said Iran had sent a message to the Oval Office following the strikes, warning Washington against taking contradictory positions.
"You cannot have your cake and eat it at the same time," he said.
He added: "You cannot ask for a ceasefire, accept the terms and conditions and the areas it applies to — including Lebanon — and then allow your ally to carry out attacks."
He said Israel's strikes amounted to "a form of genocide", while Israel has maintained it is targeting the militant group Hezbollah, which it said has been firing rockets into Israel.
Khatibzadeh said the United States "must choose" between war and peace.
"They cannot have both at the same time. They are mutually exclusive — it is quite clear," he said.
On this weekend's talks between Washington and Tehran in Pakistan, he said "back-and-forth messages" between both sides continued through Islamabad last night.
When asked whether Iran would withdraw from negotiations if Israeli strikes persist, he said: "We are very much focused on the wellbeing of the whole Middle East."
Khatibzadeh said United States Vice President JD Vance is expected to lead the American delegation, while Iran's parliamentary speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf will head Tehran's side.
When asked whether Iran would urge Hezbollah to stop firing rockets from Lebanon, he said: "We ask everyone in the Middle East to abide by the ceasefire that we reached with the Americans."
He added that under the US-Iran agreement, both sides and their respective allies were to observe the ceasefire from the outset.
When asked if Iran would instruct Hezbollah to halt attacks, Khatibzadeh described the group as a "pure Lebanese freedom movement".
Pressed on whether Iranian officers were advising, training and arming Hezbollah, he acknowledged Iran's support.
"I am not denying that," he said, adding that Iran provides "support to this freedom and resistance movement", but insisted that "they are not acting on our behalf".
On maritime security at the Strait of Hormuz, Khatibzadeh said Iran would "provide security for safe passage" through the strait, which he described as having been "open for millennia" before the conflict with the United States began.
However, he said reopening would only take place "after the United States withdraws this aggression", in apparent reference to Israeli attacks on Lebanon.
He said Iran would act in line with international norms and international law, but stressed that the strait was not in international waters, adding that safe passage depended on "the goodwill of Iran and Oman".
When asked whether safe passage meant vessels would not be charged or threatened, Khatibzadeh said Iran wanted the strait to remain "peaceful", but warned it would not allow the Gulf to be "misused by warships".
Ends
Keywords: news, NST, Malaysia, Iran, Israel, Lebanon, United States, ceasefire, Middle East, diplomacy, Hezbollah, Strait of Hormuz
From: Sein

Title: Zara Qairina's case: Trial of teacher over spreading false information resumes in June
Article:
SEPANG: The magistrate's court here today set June 11 and 12 for the continuation of the trial of an English teacher charged with causing public alarm by claiming that Zara Qairina Mahathir was put in a washing machine.
Magistrate Suriani Ujang set the dates for the trial involving Siti Hajar Aflah Sharuddin, 40, with several more witnesses expected to testify.
Deputy public prosecutors Christopher Enteri and Nadia Mohd Izhar prosecuted while lawyer Mohamad Zaiful Bahrin Kamde represented Siti Hajar Aflah.
Zaiful requested the prosecution to provide the number of witnesses to facilitate court proceedings.
Earlier, the first witness, Inspector Wong Yew Zhung, told the court that the statement made by the teacher was allegedly false and interfered with investigations.
Wong, who is the investigating officer for Zara Qairina's case, said he lodged a police report at the Kota Kinabalu International Airport police station on Aug 6 last year after seeing two videos on TikTok related to the death of the student.
On Aug 21 last year, Siti Hajar Aflah pleaded not guilty to the charge. She was accused of making a statement likely to cause public alarm on her TikTok account, "SHA_Abrienda", about 1.20pm on Aug 6 last year.
The charge, under Section 505(b) of the Penal Code, carries a maximum penalty of two years' imprisonment, a fine, or both, upon conviction.
Media previously reported that Zara Qairina, a Form 1 student at SMKA Tun Datu Mustapha in Limauan, Sabah, was found unconscious near the school's dormitory drain about 4am on July 16 last year. She died a day after being admitted to the Queen Elizabeth Hospital in Kota Kinabalu.
Claims that the victim died after being put in a washing machine went viral on social media, causing public concern, emotional distress to the family, and disrupting the police investigation.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ulu Tiram police station attack: Cop found dead still clutching firearm – witness
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The High Court was today told that one of the policemen killed in the Ulu Tiram police station attack two years ago was found dead still clutching his firearm.
Ulu Tiram police station chief  Inspector Mohd Hisyam Ezahar, 38, said that during the identification process, he saw Constable Muhamad Syafiq Ahmad Said, 24, lying dead with a head wound, still holding an HK MP5 firearm.
The second prosecution witness said Syafiq's body was found near an armoured vehicle beside the station's enquiry counter.
"When the Criminal Investigation Department (CID) asked me to identify the bodies at 5.30am on May 17, I saw Constable Ahmad Azza Fahmi Azhar, 22, lying near a drain between the barracks and the station building with severe facial lacerations.
Syafiq was lying dead with a bleeding head, and his weapon was still on his body. The male suspect's body was lying next to him," he said during examination-in-chief by deputy public prosecutor Lokman Kasim.
Recounting the incident, Hisyam, who has served as Ulu Tiram station chief since 2023, said he was at his rented home in Jalan Bestari, Ulu Tiram, when he received a distress call at 2.30am.
"I received a call from Lance Corporal Muhammad Aznel Salleh, who was panicking and breathless, informing me that a masked man armed with a parang had entered the station. I instructed the personnel on duty to contact the crime prevention patrol unit and sent a message to the station's WhatsApp group ordering all personnel to return immediately," he said.
Hisyam rushed to the station in his own vehicle. However, before entering, he spotted two of his men under a flyover opposite the station.
"I saw Aznel and Corporal Mohd Hasif Roslan. Hasif was injured and his shirt bloodied, while Aznel was in a state of panic. I told them to wait there while I made a U-turn to enter the station.
"But when I reached the gate, they warned me not to enter as the armed suspect was still inside," he said.
He regrouped with his men outside and was later informed that the suspect had seized Fahmi's weapon.
"I informed my superiors, including the district police chief, and was ordered to wait for the CID team to arrive.
"The CID team arrived at 3.30am, and I was only allowed in to identify the bodies after the area was cleared at 5.30am," he said, adding that he remained at the station until 6pm to assist the forensics team.
In the May 17 attack, Ahmad Azza Fahmi and Syafiq were killed, while Hasif, 40, survived with gunshot wounds. The attacker, identified as Radin Luqman Radin Imran, 21, was shot dead at the scene.
The incident led to the arrest of five of the attacker's family members, who are currently on trial for terrorism-related offences.
The attacker's father, Radin Imran Radin Mohd Yassin, 64, faces four charges, including instilling Islamic State (IS) ideology in his family — Radin Luqman, Radin Romyullah, Farhah Sobrina, Mariah, and his wife Rosna Jantan — at a house in Kampung Sungai Tiram between late 2014 and May 17, 2024.
The charge, framed under Section 130JG(a) of the Penal Code, carries a maximum of 30 years' imprisonment and a fine upon conviction.
He is also charged under Sections 130J(1)(a) and 130J(1)(b) of the Penal Code with supporting terrorism by possessing four homemade air rifles for IS activities. These charges carry a life sentence or a maximum of 40 years' jail, a fine, and asset forfeiture upon conviction.
Under his fourth charge, Radin Imran is accused of possessing a book related to IS titled Hakikat Islam & Hakikat Syirik by Abu Sulaiman Aman Abdurrahman, an offence under Section 130JB(1)(a) of the Penal Code.
Meanwhile, the attacker's brother, Radin Romyullah, 36, faces two charges: pledging allegiance to an IS leader and possessing a hard drive containing IS materials, under Section 130JB(1)(a) of the Penal Code.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MetMalaysia issues thunderstorm warning for seven states until 5pm today
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a thunderstorm warning, including heavy rain and strong winds, for seven states until 5pm today.
The affected states are Kedah, Perak, Kelantan, Terengganu, Pahang, Johor, and Sabah.
In Kedah, the areas affected are Padang Terap, Sik, and Baling, while in Perak, the warning covers Hulu Perak, Kuala Kangsar, Manjung, Kinta, Kampar, Batang Padang, and Muallim.
For Kelantan, the alert includes Jeli, Machang, and Pasir Puteh, while in Terengganu, it affects Besut, Setiu, Hulu Terengganu, Dungun, and Kemaman.
In Pahang, the areas expected to be hit are Cameron Highlands, Lipis, Raub, Jerantut, Bentong, Kuantan, and Pekan, while Mersing in Johor is also affected.
In Sabah, the warning covers areas such as Sipitang, Beaufort, Tambunan, Papar, Putatan, Penampang, Kota Kinabalu, and Tuaran.
MetMalaysia said there are signs of thunderstorms with rainfall exceeding 20mm per hour that are ongoing or expected to persist for more than an hour.
A thunderstorm warning is a short-term alert, valid for no more than six hours per issuance.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: War in the Middle East: latest developments
Article:
PARIS, (France): The latest developments in the Middle East war:
- Spain embassy -
Spain will reopen its embassy in Iran, which was closed in March due to the war, Spanish Foreign Minister Jose Manuel Albares said.
- China on Lebanon -
A spokesman for the Chinese foreign ministry said "Lebanon's sovereignty and security should not be violated. The safety of civilian lives and property must be guaranteed" and that Beijing was urging "a cooling down of the regional situation."
- UK on Lebanon -
British Foreign Minister Yvette Cooper said "we do strongly want to see the ceasefire extended to Lebanon."
"I'm deeply troubled about the escalating attacks that we saw from Israel in Lebanon yesterday," she told Sky News.
- France on Lebanon -
France's foreign minister, Jean-Noel Barrot, condemned Israel's continuing strikes on Lebanon, calling them "all the more unacceptable as they undermine the temporary ceasefire reached yesterday between the United States and Iran."
- Lebanon day of mourning -
Lebanese Prime Minister Nawaf Salam declared Thursday a national day of mourning after Israeli strikes pummelled the country, killing at least 182 people and wounding 890, according to the health ministry.
The strikes were Israel's heaviest on neighbouring Lebanon — including in densely packed central Beirut — since the Iran-backed militant group Hizbollah joined the war in early March.
- Hizbollah fires at Israel -
Iran-backed militant group Hizbollah said it had fired rockets towards Israel in response to its "violation" of the US-Iran truce.
It came a day after the Lebanese group said it has a "right" to respond to a deadly wave of Israeli strikes across Lebanon.
- Alternative Hormuz routes -
Iran announced alternative routes for ships travelling through the Strait of Hormuz, citing the risk of sea mines in the main zone of the vital waterway.
The statement shared instructions for an alternative entry and exit route through the strait.
- 'Outraged' -
The Red Cross said it was "outraged by the devastating death and destruction" caused by Israeli strikes in densely populated areas across Lebanon on Wednesday.
"People across Lebanon were holding their breath for a ceasefire agreement, but a wave of deadly strikes plunged the country into panic and chaos," said Agnes Dhur, the ICRC's head of delegation in Lebanon.
- Macron on Lebanon -
French President Emmanuel Macron urged US President Donald Trump and Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian to include Lebanon in the ceasefire the two sides had reached.
"I expressed my hope that the ceasefire will be fully respected by each of the belligerents, across all areas of confrontation, including in Lebanon," Macron wrote on X after speaking with both leaders.
- 'Relieved' -
Some in the Iranian capital feared the ceasefire will come to naught, but others declared victory and most breathed a sigh of relief after weeks of bombardment.
"Everyone is at ease now, we are more relaxed," Sakineh Mohammadi, a 50-year-old housewife, told AFP, saying she was "proud" of her country.
- Iran demands Lebanon truce -
Pezeshkian said a halt to Israeli attacks in Lebanon was one of the key conditions of the Islamic Republic's 10-point plan for securing an end to the Middle East war, state media reported Wednesday.
- Vance to Islamabad -
US Vice President JD Vance will lead talks with Iran beginning Saturday in Islamabad, and will be joined by special envoy Steve Witkoff and Trump's son-in-law Jared Kushner, the White House said.
Speaking to reporters in Hungary, Vance urged Iran not to let the truce fall apart over Lebanon, insisting that a halt to Israel's fighting there had never been a part of the agreement reached the night before.
- UN outrage -
The UN rights chief and the International Committee of the Red Cross (ICRC) voiced outrage over Israel's attacks on Lebanon.
"The scale of the killing and destruction in Lebanon today is nothing short of horrific," UN High Commissioner for Human Rights Volker Turk said.
"Such carnage, within hours of agreeing to a ceasefire with Iran, defies belief."
The ICRC said it was "outraged by the devastating death and destruction."

The safety of civilian lives and property must be guaranteed" and that Beijing was urging "a cooling down of the regional situation."
